channel.title: Azure VMware Solution on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in Azure VMware Solution on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: azure: Retirement: Azure VMware Solution AV36 node End of Support on September 30, 2027
item.description:
Microsoft says the Azure VMware Solution AV36 node will reach end of support on September 30, 2027, because Broadcom will end support for the current AV36 VCF version on that date. AV36 is also not compatible with VMware Cloud Foundation 9.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Support for workloads with large files in Azure NetApp Files
item.description:
Azure NetApp Files now supports regular volumes with file sizes up to 64 TiB. This is intended for workloads that use large files, including Azure VMware Solution virtual machines with large VMDK disks.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: AVS Support for AV64 SKU on Azure Elastic SAN
item.description:
Azure Elastic SAN datastores are now generally available with AV64 SKUs for Azure VMware Solution. This adds a higher-scale, higher-performance storage option for those deployments.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Retirement: Azure VMware Solution AV36P and AV52 node retirement on June 30, 2029
item.description:
Microsoft will retire Azure VMware Solution AV36P and AV52 nodes on June 30, 2029. Existing AV36P and AV52 Reserved Instance terms are not affected, but customers should review RI expirations and plan migration before the retirement date.

  • Source: Azure Updates
